    1. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
    1. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
      1. is true but (R) is false.
      1. is false but (R) is true.

28. Arrange the following concepts in order in which they evolved. Select the correct answer from the codes given below.

I. Principle of Uniformitarianism II. Theory of Continental Drift III. Dynamic Equilibrium Theory IV. Theory of Convection Current

Codes:

29. When two meander necks do not come closer but a channel from one neck joins to other and the main flow turns on that channel is termed as

    1. Parallel channel flow
    1. Semi-neck cut off
    1. Chute cut off
    1. Non-neck cut off

30. Superimposed profile is drawn to understand the

    1. break of slope.
    1. ruggedness of topography.
    1. panoramic view.
    1. cyclic nature of landscape.

31. In a periglacial environment, pingos form due to

    1. Ice wedge coalescence
    1. Ice core growth
    1. Ice action
    1. Ice penetration from active zone

32. Trunks of trees growing on the banks of natural water bodies are often seen to take U-shaped bends. This primarily occurs due to

    1. Phototropism
    1. Gravitropism
    1. Water-level fluctuations
    1. Creep of the bank materials

33. Assertion (A): Portions of glacial troughs may exhibit remarkably flat floors. Reason (R): The flat floor in a glacial trough is produced by uniform glacial erosion.

    1. Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
    1. Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
      1. is true but (R) is false.
      1. is false but (R) is true.

34. Transportation cost does not play a major role in the location of textile industry, because

    1. cotton is a weight loosing material.
    1. cotton is a weight gaining material.
    1. weight loss in cotton during manufacturing is very high.
    1. weight of cotton is more or less equal to the weight of the finished product.

35. Which one of the following methods is considered as the segregated set of relationship among the factors of an event?

    1. Flow Analysis
    1. Principal Component Analysis
    1. Weighted Index
    1. Gravity Analysis

Section 5: Biogeography & Statistics (Q36–Q41)

36. Ecotone is an area that can be depicted by quantitative method through

    1. Neutral Set
    1. Singular Set
    1. Intersection Set
    1. Union Set

37. Consider the following statements: Short duration leptokurtic storm hydrograph is produced at the outlet of a drainage basin when

  1. the shape of the basin is circular.
  2. the intensity of rainfall is very high.
  3. the average slope is less.
  4. the drainage density is high.

Which of the above statements is correct?

    1. 1 and 2
    1. 3 and 4
    1. 1 and 3
    1. 2 and 3

38. In a socio-economic survey when data is collected through a questionnaire in the form of a natural order like — high, medium and low, such data is classified as

    1. Nominal Data
    1. Ordinal Data
    1. Interval Data
    1. Ratio Data

39. Law of stream number confirms to the mathematical model of

    1. Positive Exponential Function
    1. Negative Exponential Function
    1. Positive Arithmatic Function
    1. Negative Arithmatic Function

40. In articulating the idea of ‘Possibilism’ La Blache was influenced by the idea of

    1. Cosmos of Humboldt
    1. Anthropogeography of Ratzel
    1. Erdkunde of Ritter
    1. The Morphology of Landscape of Sauer

41. Darwinism in Social Ecology was first applied by

    1. W. M. Davis
    1. T. H. Huxley
    1. H. Spencer
    1. Carl O. Sauer

Section 6: Geographical Thought & Philosophy (Q42–Q46)

42. The social theory that appeared as the sequel to Behaviouralism is

    1. Radicalism
    1. Structuralism
    1. Game Theory
    1. Feminism

43. La Terre is authored by whom of the following?

    1. Réclus
    1. Kropotkin
    1. Ritter
    1. Huxley

44. Who used ‘Grand Theory’ for the first time?

    1. D. Harvey
    1. M. Foucault
    1. C. W. Mills
    1. T. Parsons

45. Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) was first introduced in which country?

    1. Canada
    1. Sweden
    1. Norway
    1. U.S.A.

46. Scholastic relationship under bivariate analysis is primarily a

    1. Probable relationship
    1. Estimated relationship
    1. Linear relationship
    1. Non-linear relationship

Section 7: Agriculture & Economic Geography (Q47–Q50)

47. The major agricultural regions of the world were first identified by

    1. Johann Heinrich von Thunen
    1. Derwent Wittlesey
    1. William J. Peters
    1. David Grigg

48. Which of the following correctly describes the characteristics of deep ecology?

  1. Holistic view
  2. Anthropocentric environmentalism
  3. Green movements
  4. Simple living

Select the correct answer from the codes given below:

    1. 1, 2 and 3
    1. 1, 3 and 4
    1. 2, 3 and 4
    1. 1, 2 and 4

49. Field capacity with reference to soil-water-plant relationships refers to

    1. Capillary water
    1. Capillary and hygroscopic water
    1. Capillary, hygroscopic and confined water
    1. Capillary, hygroscopic, confined water and water vapour

50. Match List-I with List-II.

List-I (Geologic Periods/Epochs)

    1. Triassic
    1. Jurassic
    1. Pleistocene
    1. Pliocene

List-II (Life Forms)

    1. Origin of man
    1. Rise of birds and flying reptiles
    1. Rise of archaic animals
    1. Rise of dinosaurs
(a) (b) (c) (d)
(A) (iv) (ii) (iii) (i)
(B) (i) (ii) (iii) (iv)
(C) (i) (iv) (iii) (ii)
(D) (iii) (iv) (ii) (i)

Section 8: Fluvial & Oceanography (Q51–Q60)

51. Due to presence of natural levee of main stream, tributary of the main stream sometimes may flow parallel to the main stream before joining. This is called

    1. Anabranching stream
    1. Braided channel
    1. Yazoo stream
    1. Anastomosing channel

52. Which of the following geological era is associated with the folding of the Himalayan mountain system?

    1. Caenozoic
    1. Mesozoic
    1. Palaeozoic
    1. Azoic

53. Match the following Pressure Belts with Latitudes:

Pressure Belts

    1. Equatorial Low Pressure Belt
    1. Subtropical High Pressure Belt
    1. Subpolar Low Pressure Belt
    1. Polar High Pressure Belt

Latitudes

    1. 25°–35° North and South
    1. 5°–5° North and South
    1. 60°–65° North and South
    1. 85°–90° North and South
(a) (b) (c) (d)
(A) (2) (1) (3) (4)
(B) (1) (2) (3) (4)
(C) (3) (4) (2) (1)
(D) (4) (3) (2) (1)

54. In which of the following continents the range of temperatures ever recorded had been the minimum?

    1. Asia
    1. Africa
    1. Australia
    1. Antarctica

55. ‘Fordism’ is used to describe

    1. manufacturing technique only.
    1. societal condition only.
    1. Neither (A) nor (B)
    1. Both (A) and (B)

56. The ‘γ’ value of a correlation coefficient varies between

    1. +1.0 to −1.0
    1. 0 to +1.0
    1. −1.0 to 0.0
    1. 1.00 to 10.0

57. Which one of the following pairs is incorrect?

    1. Isobar – Pressure
    1. Isotherm – Temperature
    1. Isohels – Wind Velocity
    1. Isohytes – Rainfall

58. The Hill Areas Development Programme in India was initiated during

    1. the Seventh 5 Year Plan Period.
    1. the Second 5 Year Plan Period.
    1. the Fourth 5 Year Plan Period.
    1. the Fifth 5 Year Plan Period.

59. The growth centre programme to promote industrialisation of backward areas in India was introduced in which year?

    1. 1951
    1. 1973
    1. 1988
    1. 1991

60. The data of estimation of India’s National Income is issued by

    1. Census of India
    1. National Data Centre
    1. Central Statistical Organisation
    1. NITI Aayog

Section 9: Indian Economy & Regional Development (Q61–Q70)

61. The Second Five Year Plan in India was based on

    1. Herold-Domar Model
    1. P. C. Mahalanobis Model
    1. V. T. Stanberry Model
    1. A. R. Kuklinski Model

62. Which one of the following is not the essential characteristics of post-Fordism?

    1. Reliance on e-transaction of capital
    1. Reliance on heavy industries
    1. Reliance on market linked production
    1. Reliance on share market

63. The theory of Mobility Transition Model is conditioned by

    1. birth rate, death rate and migration of population of a region.
    1. birth rate and death rate of a region.
    1. population growth, level of urbanization and change of technology.
    1. import, export and capital flow.

64. The abstract belief systems that pass over from generation to generation is called

    1. Artifact
    1. Mentifact
    1. Sociofact
    1. Cultural diffusion

65. Which of the following characterises Mixed Farming?

    1. Practice of cultivation of crops and raising livestock simultaneously.
    1. Practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously in the same field.
    1. Practice of growing different crops in succession in the same field.
    1. Practice of raising alternate crops successively for subsistence and marketing.

66. Which of the following activities is performed quite close to the market as per Von Thunen’s model of Agricultural Location?

    1. Extensive grain farming
    1. Dairying and market gardening
    1. Mixed farming
    1. Extensive stock raising

67. The classic essay “The Tragedy of the Commons” was written by

    1. David Harvey
    1. Charles Darwin
    1. David Smith
    1. Garrett Hardin

68. The ‘Multiple Nuclei Model’ states that

    1. urban land use shows a series of circular belts around the city centre.
    1. cities do not develop any recognizable zones of land use.
    1. wedge-shaped zones radiate outward from the central business district.
    1. cities develop several nodes of growth each with specialised use.

69. Who introduced the idea of ‘Garden City’?

    1. Le Corbusier
    1. Robert Owen
    1. Ebenezer Howard
    1. Frank Lloyd Wright

70. Which among the following is odd?

    1. Mercantile model : Vance
    1. Metropolitan evolution : Borchert
    1. Selective urban growth : Muller
    1. Central Place theory : Christaller

Section 10: Settlement & Population Geography (Q71–Q81)

71. As per the classification of towns by the Census of India 2011, Class-III towns have a population of

    1. 50,000 to 99,999
    1. 20,000 to 49,999
    1. 10,000 to 19,999
    1. 5,000 to 9,999

72. Which one of the following rural settlements has the best road network?

    1. Street village
    1. Peri-urban village
    1. Transit village
    1. Cruciform village

73. Which one is considered as the best combination as precondition of pre-industrial urban growth?

    1. Population – Environment – Technology – Social organization
    1. Population – Political organization – Social Organization – Technology – Trade
    1. Population – Environment – Technology – Trade
    1. Population – Trade – Transport – Political Organization

74. Growth of population through the successive stage of ‘Demographic Transition Model’ has the appearance of a

    1. Geometric curve
    1. Exponential curve
    1. Sigmoid curve
    1. Parabolic curve

75. ‘Fecundity’ is

    1. Capacity to reproduce
    1. Actual reproductive performance
    1. Actual births taken place
    1. Inability to reproduce

76. The Boserupian theory of population growth is most appropriate for which society?

    1. All societies
    1. Developed societies
    1. Subsistence societies
    1. Bourgeoise society

77. As per the United Nations, when did the world population reach the one billion mark for the first time?

    1. 1787
    1. 1804
    1. 1857
    1. 1927

78. For small distance migration, female migration overtakes male due to

    1. demographic structure of the society.
    1. occupational structure of the society.
    1. economic structure of the society.
    1. patriarchal structure of the society.

79. Which district of West Bengal among the following has the highest concentration of Sabaras?

    1. Purulia
    1. Bankura
    1. Paschim Medinipur
    1. Jhargram

80. Two-thirds of the world population live in

    1. the northern hemisphere.
    1. the areas between 20° and 50° South.
    1. the areas between 20° and 60° North.
    1. the temperate areas of the world.

81. The third stage of demographic transition is marked by

    1. declining birth rate and low population growth.
    1. high birth and death rates.
    1. low birth and death rates.
    1. high natural increase of population.

Section 11: Oceanography (Q82–Q86)

82. In which part of the oceans and seas oozes are quite uncommon in their occurrences?

    1. Abyssal plain
    1. Oceanic deeps
    1. Summits of sea mounts
    1. Submarine canyons

83. Which of the following types of tides have the highest amplitude given the other factors remaining the same?

    1. Spring tides
    1. Perigean neap tides
    1. Perigean spring tides
    1. Apogean spring tides

84. The CaCO₃ compensation depth in seas is usually about

    1. 4,000 m
    1. 4,500 m
    1. 5,000 m
    1. 5,500 m

85. The average global rate of sea-level rise at present is about

    1. 1.6 mm/yr
    1. 2.1 mm/yr
    1. 2.6 mm/yr
    1. 3.3 mm/yr

86. Past records of sea-level changes are best deciphered from

    1. Sandy coasts
    1. Rocky coasts
    1. Mangrove-fringed coasts
    1. Ice-fringed coasts

Section 12: Political Geography & Geomorphology (Q87–Q93)

87. The concept of ‘Lebensraum’ in Political Geography refers to

    1. a living space sought for occupation by a nation with expanding population.
    1. a territory not occupied by any nation.
    1. a buffer zone between two nations.
    1. a boundary area with rich natural resources.

88. A continuous spiral motion of water as it flows along a river channel is termed as

    1. Helical flow
    1. Laminar flow
    1. Steady flow
    1. Base flow

89. Residual Hums landscape feature is found in

    1. Karst topography
    1. Desert landscape
    1. Glacio-fluvial landscape
    1. Areas of marine transgression

90. Shingles are the water-worn rounded stones, usually associated with

    1. Fluvial process
    1. Glacial process
    1. Aeolian process
    1. Marine process

91. The time interval between two steady state or equilibrium conditions is called

    1. Relaxation time
    1. Steady time
    1. Geosync time
    1. Alteration time

92. Compared to true dip, the value of apparent dip of a sedimentary stratum is

    1. either higher or lower.
    1. always higher.
    1. always lower.
    1. different by 90° in strike direction.

93. Lahars are best described as mass-movement features associated with

    1. Volcanoes
    1. Mud banks
    1. Valley sidewalls
    1. Landslide scars

Section 13: Geographical Thought & Political Geography (Q94–Q100)

94. The Globe was divided into ‘geostrategic realms’ by whom?

    1. Bassin
    1. Cohen
    1. Kjellen
    1. Parker

95. The ‘Rimland Theory’ was propounded by whom among the following?

    1. Bernard Grofman
    1. Richard Morrill
    1. Martin Glassner
    1. Nicholas Spykman

96. Which of the following are basic elements in Peter Haggett’s model for the Study of Spatial Systems? (I) Channels (II) Diffusion (III) Hierarchies (IV) Movements (V) Surfaces

Codes:

      1. and (II)
    1. (I), (II) and (IV)
      1. only
    1. (I), (II), (III), (IV) and (V)

97. The term ‘Geopiety’ was coined by whom?

    1. C. O. Sauer
    1. A. Buttimer
    1. J. K. Wright
    1. Yi-Fu Tuan

98. Arrange the following binaries and identify the correct option:

Column A Column B
a. General I. Emotional
b. Explanation II. Complexity
c. Objectivity III. Particular
d. Order IV. Interpretation
e. Rational V. Subjectivity
    1. a–II, b–I, c–III, d–V, e–IV
    1. a–V, b–IV, c–III, d–II, e–I
    1. a–III, b–IV, c–V, d–II, e–I
    1. a–I, b–III, c–V, d–IV, e–II

99. Empiricism is associated with whom?

    1. John Locke
    1. David Hume
    1. August Comte
    1. Edmund Husserl

100. S. B. Jones’ view of states was based on the idea of

    1. Deutsch, Gottman and Hartshorne
    1. Mackinder, Gottman and Hartshorne
    1. Deutsch, Gottman and Mackinder
    1. Ratzel, Hartshorne and Johnston
